summary: Comprehensive testing of GTI Fleet Management System code review vulnerability fixes - Iteration 6. All fixes verified working. Backend: 10/10 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All pages load correctly with no console errors.

backend_api_tests: GET /api/tco/fleet - returns 200 with TCO data (variable shadowing fix verified)
frontend_ui_tests: Login page loads correctly with golden theme
Driver login works - redirects to Personal Dashboard
Group Fleet Manager login shows OTP verification form
Staff Dashboard loads with all stat cards (27 vehicles, 26 drivers)
Country filter dropdown visible for Group Manager
Fleet Map page loads with country cards and vehicle locations
Vehicles page loads with search and country filter
Reports & TCO page loads with expense breakdown and utilization
Fuel Management page loads with transaction table
Maintenance Requests page loads with 'Submitted By' column and role badges
Driver Logbook page loads with entries table
Pre-Trip Checklist page loads with driver/vehicle selection
Driving Metrics page loads with safety metrics and driving score
Sidebar navigation works with correct menu items
No console.log/console.error statements in browser console
code_review_fixes_verified: Console statements removed from frontend code
Index-as-key replaced with stable keys in Sidebar.js
React hook dependencies properly declared with useCallback
Test file uses os.environ.get() for credentials
Backend variable shadowing fixed in /api/tco/fleet endpoint

group_manager.note: Requires OTP verification - check backend logs or MongoDB otp_codes collection

context_for_next_testing_agent: All code review vulnerability fixes verified working: 1) Console statements removed from all frontend files, 2) Index-as-key replaced with stable keys (divider-${item.label}, item.path) in Sidebar.js, 3) React hook dependencies properly declared using useCallback in AuthContext, VehicleMap, Reports, Dashboard, Fuel, DriverLogbook, DrivingMetrics, MaintenanceRequests, PreTripChecklist, Vehicles, 4) Test file test_country_scoped_dashboard.py uses os.environ.get() for credentials with fallback values, 5) Backend server.py variable shadowing fixed (v renamed to vehicle in /api/tco/fleet). Token storage still uses localStorage (noted as insecure but functional). OTP flow for Group Fleet Manager works correctly.

console_statements_removed.details: No console.log/console.error statements found in frontend pages, contexts, or components

index_as_key_fixed.details: Sidebar.js uses stable keys: divider-${item.label} for dividers, item.path for menu items

react_hook_dependencies.details: All useCallback hooks properly declared with dependencies in AuthContext, VehicleMap, Reports, Dashboard, Fuel, DriverLogbook, DrivingMetrics, MaintenanceRequests, PreTripChecklist, Vehicles

test_file_secrets.details: test_country_scoped_dashboard.py uses os.environ.get() with fallback values for GROUP_MANAGER_EMAIL, GROUP_MANAGER_PASSWORD, DRIVER_EMAIL, DRIVER_PASSWORD

backend_variable_shadowing.details: /api/tco/fleet endpoint works correctly - variable 'v' renamed to 'vehicle' to avoid shadowing

login_flow.details: Driver login works directly. Group Fleet Manager login requires OTP verification via email (Resend service)

all_pages_load.details: Dashboard, Fleet Map, Vehicles, Drivers, Logbook, Driving Metrics, Pre-Trip Checklist, Maintenance, Requests, Fuel, Reports all load correctly

notes.token_storage: Token is stored in localStorage (noted as insecure in code review but functional). Consider migrating to httpOnly cookies for production.
notes.otp_verification: Group Fleet Manager login requires OTP. OTP is sent via Resend email service and stored in MongoDB otp_codes collection. For testing, retrieve OTP from database or backend logs.
